mathematical_rationale: The corrected Cut-and-Paste property for two-way Turing machines states that if two input pairs (x1,y1) and (x2,y2) produce identical crossing sequences at the tape boundary, then swapping the right halves yields a composite input (x1,y2) that the machine cannot distinguish from the original left pair (x1,y1). Formally: CS(x1,y1) = CS(x2,y2) implies Out(x1,y2) = Out(x1,y1). For the diagonal fooling set S = {(a,a) | a in FRSB_Information_Cut k}, assume for distinct a,b that CS(a,a) = CS(b,b). Applying the property with x1:=a, y1:=a, x2:=b, y2:=b gives Out(a,b) = Out(a,a). By correctness of FRSB_Target, Out(a,a) = true and Out(a,b) = false, yielding false = true, a contradiction. Hence CS must be injective on S, requiring at least 2^k distinct crossing sequences.
lean_4_code_delta:
import Mathlib.Data.Fintype.Card

variable {CrossingSeq : Type*}
  (compute_crossing : FRSB_Information_Cut k → FRSB_Information_Cut k → CrossingSeq)
  (output_of_pair : FRSB_Information_Cut k → FRSB_Information_Cut k → Bool)
  (cut_paste_property : ∀ (x1 x2 y1 y2 : FRSB_Information_Cut k),
    compute_crossing x1 y1 = compute_crossing x2 y2 →
    output_of_pair x1 y2 = output_of_pair x1 y1)

/-- Using the corrected Cut-and-Paste property, prove that crossing sequences for distinct diagonal
    elements are distinct. -/
lemma FRSB_CutAndPaste_Crossing_Sequence_Bound (k : ℕ)
    (h_correct : ∀ (x y : FRSB_Information_Cut k), output_of_pair x y = FRSB_Target k x y) :
    ∀ (p q ∈ FRSB_Diagonal_Finset k), p ≠ q →
      compute_crossing p.1 p.2 ≠ compute_crossing q.1 q.2 := by
  intro p hp q hq h_ne
  -- Extract the first components a and b from the diagonal pairs (a,a) and (b,b).
  rcases Finset.mem_image.mp hp with ⟨a, _, rfl⟩
  rcases Finset.mem_image.mp hq with ⟨b, _, rfl⟩
  have hab : a ≠ b := by
    contrapose h_ne
    simp [h_ne]
  intro h_cross_eq
  -- Apply the corrected Cut-and-Paste property.
  have h_out_eq : output_of_pair a b = output_of_pair a a := by
    apply cut_paste_property (x1 := a) (y1 := a) (x2 := b) (y2 := b)
    exact h_cross_eq
  -- Correctness of FRSB_Target yields the contradiction.
  rw [h_correct a b, h_correct a a] at h_out_eq
  simp [FRSB_Target, hab] at h_out_eq

/-- Corollary: The crossing sequence mapping restricted to the diagonal is injective. -/
lemma FRSB_CrossingSeq_Injective_On_Diagonal (k : ℕ)
    (h_correct : ∀ (x y : FRSB_Information_Cut k), output_of_pair x y = FRSB_Target k x y) :
    ∃ f : FRSB_Diagonal_Finset k → CrossingSeq, Function.Injective f := by
  refine ⟨fun p => compute_crossing p.val.1 p.val.2, ?_⟩
  intro p q h_eq
  contrapose h_eq
  intro h_ne
  exact FRSB_CutAndPaste_Crossing_Sequence_Bound k compute_crossing output_of_pair
    cut_paste_property h_correct p.val p.prop q.val q.prop h_ne

/-- Lower bound: At least 2^k distinct crossing sequences are required. -/
lemma FRSB_CrossingSeq_LowerBound (k : ℕ) [Fintype CrossingSeq]
    (h_correct : ∀ (x y : FRSB_Information_Cut k), output_of_pair x y = FRSB_Target k x y) :
    2 ^ k ≤ Fintype.card CrossingSeq := by
  have ⟨f, hf_inj⟩ := FRSB_CrossingSeq_Injective_On_Diagonal k compute_crossing
    output_of_pair cut_paste_property h_correct
  rw [← FRSB_Diagonal_card k]
  apply Fintype.card_le_of_injective f hf_inj
expected_verifier_state: The Lean kernel accepts the code with the corrected cut_paste_property. The proof of FRSB_CutAndPaste_Crossing_Sequence_Bound correctly instantiates x1:=a, y1:=a, x2:=b, y2:=b to derive output_of_pair a b = output_of_pair a a, leading to contradiction via correctness. No 'sorry' or 'axiom' is used, and all definitions (FRSB_Information_Cut, FRSB_Target, FRSB_Diagonal_Finset) are assumed from prior context.
